title: Per-Origin Politeness, Concurrency, Retry Classification, and Adaptive Rate
description: A global sleep still sends bursts per host, retries permanent blocks, ignores Retry-After, synchronizes workers, and increases load after slow responses. Learners cumulatively produce per-origin politeness and retry controller through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Schedule requests by effective origin with minimum start spacing, stable crawler identity, robots and owner policy, and monotonic time.
Limit active work per origin and globally while accounting for redirects, browser resources, retries, and queued fairness.
Retry only transient, safe, replayable failures under an attempt and elapsed-time budget and stop on denial or policy signals.
